skill.name: bamboohr-automation
skill.description: Automate BambooHR human resources operations through Composio's BambooHR toolkit via Rube MCP
skill.summary: Execute BambooHR workflows including employee management, time-off requests, benefits administration, and dependent tracking through MCP-connected tools
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 61 patterns that are all false positives. The 52 external_commands detections are markdown backticks for tool names, not shell execution. The network finding is a legitimate MCP endpoint URL. Cryptographic algorithm findings reference no actual crypto code. This documentation-only skill safely connects to BambooHR via standard MCP protocol.

content.user_title: Automate BambooHR HR operations with AI
content.value_statement: Streamline human resources workflows by connecting AI assistants to BambooHR through MCP. Execute employee searches, time-off management, and benefits tracking without manual portal navigation.
actual_capabilities: Retrieve and search employee directory with customizable field selection
Track employee data changes since a specified timestamp for incremental sync
Manage time-off requests including balance checks, submissions, and approvals
Update employee profile information with audit trail preservation
Access dependent and benefits coverage data for enrolled employees
limitations: Requires active Rube MCP connection with authenticated BambooHR credentials
Field names and available operations depend on BambooHR account permissions
Cannot access read-only fields or perform operations requiring higher privileges than the API key allows
Rate limits imposed by BambooHR API may affect bulk operations

use_cases.title: HR Manager Dashboard
use_cases.description: Query employee directories, approve pending time-off requests, and generate change reports for weekly team meetings

use_cases.title: Payroll Sync Automation
use_cases.description: Detect employee changes since last payroll run and update external payroll systems with modified employee data

use_cases.title: Employee Self-Service Assistant
use_cases.description: Enable employees to check their time-off balances, submit vacation requests, and view their dependents through natural language queries

prompt_templates.title: Find Employee Contact
prompt_templates.prompt: Search the employee directory for someone in the Engineering department and return their name, email, and job title

prompt_templates.title: Time-Off Request Submission
prompt_templates.prompt: Check my available vacation balance, then submit a time-off request from 2026-03-15 to 2026-03-20 for 5 days with the note 'Family vacation'

prompt_templates.title: Incremental Employee Sync
prompt_templates.prompt: Get all employees who have been updated since 2026-02-01T00:00:00Z, retrieve their full profiles, and list the changed fields for each

prompt_templates.title: Bulk Time-Off Approval
prompt_templates.prompt: List all pending time-off requests for next month, then approve all requests from the Marketing department and deny any that exceed 10 days

output_examples.input: Find all employees in the Sales department
output_examples.output: Employee directory query returns 12 Sales team members with fields: firstName, lastName, workEmail, jobTitle, and status. Results include active sales representatives and one recently terminated employee flagged by status field.

output_examples.input: Submit vacation request for John Smith, March 10-14, 2026
output_examples.output: Time-off request created successfully with ID 4892. Request shows employeeId 156, timeOffTypeId 3 (Vacation), start date 2026-03-10, end date 2026-03-14, amount 5 days. Status is pending manager approval.

best_practices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to retrieve current tool schemas before executing workflows
Resolve employee names to numeric IDs via GET_ALL_EMPLOYEES before performing detailed operations
Store and reuse timestamps for incremental sync patterns to avoid redundant API calls
anti_patterns: Do not call GET_EMPLOYEE for each employee in a loop when GET_ALL_EMPLOYEES provides directory data more efficiently
Avoid submitting time-off requests without first checking available balances via GET_TIME_OFF_BALANCES
Never hardcode employee IDs or time-off type IDs; always resolve them dynamically from current data

faq.question: What do I need before using this skill?
faq.answer: You need Rube MCP connected in your AI client and an active BambooHR connection. Run RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS with toolkit 'bamboohr' to authenticate if not already active.

faq.question: How do I find an employee's ID?
faq.answer: Call BAMBOOHR_GET_ALL_EMPLOYEES to retrieve the directory, then search results by name. The employee ID is a numeric field used for detailed operations.

faq.question: Can I automatically approve time-off requests?
faq.answer: Yes, if your BambooHR API key has manager or admin permissions. Use UPDATE_TIME_OFF_REQUEST with status 'approved' after retrieving the requestId.

faq.question: What date formats does BambooHR expect?
faq.answer: Time-off dates use 'YYYY-MM-DD' format. Change detection timestamps use ISO 8601 format with timezone like '2026-02-01T00:00:00Z'.

faq.question: Are terminated employees included in search results?
faq.answer: Yes, GET_ALL_EMPLOYEES may include inactive employees. Check the 'status' field to filter for active employees only.

faq.question: How do I keep external systems synced with BambooHR?
faq.answer: Use the incremental sync pattern: store last_sync_timestamp, call EMPLOYEE_GET_CHANGED with that timestamp, fetch details for changed employees, then update your timestamp.
